title: dianthus alpinus var. repens
intro: Dianthus alpinus var. repens, also known as creeping alpine pink, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds and fever, as its roots and leaves contain antiseptic and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Dianthus alpinus var. repens include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and soothe skin irritations.
